fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 67 uses strict inequalities (`end <= s` and `start >= e`), which incorrectly allows adjacent intervals (where end==s or start==e) to be accepted instead of rejected.

spec.expr: not (end__1 == s) and not (start__1 == e) or not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e)

constraints.intent: The overlap check must treat touching boundaries as overlapping; if end==s or start==e the condition should detect a conflict, so the non-overlap guard must use strict inequalities in the opposite sense (end < s or start > e).
After unpacking the validated interval, end must be strictly greater than start.
At the end of each loop iteration, if the new interval merely touches an existing slot boundary it should have already triggered a conflict return; touching is not allowed.
When the overlap condition is not triggered (else branch), the new interval must be strictly non-adjacent to the existing slot, meaning end < s or start > e.
